findings.evidence: `smul_eq_apply` at line 93 is `@[simp]` and rewrites `φ • e` to `φ.1 e`; there is no `@[simp]` lemma that then rewrites `p (φ.1 e)` to `p e`.
findings.issue: `map_proj` lacks `@[simp]`. Because `smul_eq_apply` is `@[simp]`, a `simp` call on `p (φ • e)` reduces to `p (φ.1 e)` and stops; `map_proj` is needed to complete the reduction to `p e`.

findings.evidence: Roadmap Stage 0.4 lists `Subgroup`, `MulAction`, `FaithfulSMul`, `ContinuousConstSMul` as deliverables, not a fiber homeomorphism. `grep fiberHomeomorph` finds only apply/symm_apply lemmas; no mul/inv interaction lemmas exist.
findings.fix: Either mark `fiberHomeomorph` (and its `mapsTo_fiber*` helpers) `private`, or add `fiberHomeomorph_mul : fiberHomeomorph (φ * ψ) b = (fiberHomeomorph φ b).trans (fiberHomeomorph ψ b)` and `fiberHomeomorph_inv : fiberHomeomorph (φ⁻¹) b = (fiberHomeomorph φ b).symm`.
findings.issue: `fiberHomeomorph` is public but the file has no `fiberHomeomorph_mul` or `fiberHomeomorph_inv` lemmas. A consumer building Stage 1 (`Deck(proj) ≃* π₁`) must unfold the definition to verify it is a group homomorphism. Stage 0.4 in the roadmap does not name a fiber homeomorphism, so the surface is over-exposed relative to the stated target.

summary: `map_proj` is missing `@[simp]`, leaving `p (φ • e)` unreduced after `smul_eq_apply` fires. `fiberHomeomorph` is public but has no group-operation characterization (`fiberHomeomorph_mul`, `fiberHomeomorph_inv`), so its API is incomplete for any consumer who needs to treat it as a group homomorphism without unfolding.
